meta.title: About | ProxMenux Documentation
meta.description: About ProxMenux: project goals, contributors, FAQ and Code of Conduct. Open-source menu-driven management tool for Proxmox VE 8+, built and maintained by MacRimi with a small team of testers and contributors.
meta.ogDescription: About ProxMenux — open-source menu-driven management tool for Proxmox VE 8+.

header.title: About
header.description: Project info, FAQ, contributors and code of conduct. ProxMenux is an open-source menu-driven management tool for Proxmox VE 8+, built and maintained by MacRimi with the help of a small team of testers and contributors.

callout.title: Open source, community-driven
callout.body: ProxMenux is GPL-3.0 licensed and developed in the open on GitHub. Every script is reviewable, every change traceable. Contributions — bug reports, ideas, code, testing — are welcome.

options.title: FAQ
options.description: Frequently asked questions: what ProxMenux is, install, compatibility, customisation, updates, security, production use, uninstall.

options.title: Contributors
options.description: Recognition for the people who make ProxMenux possible — testers, reviewers and developers who shape each release.

options.title: Code of Conduct
options.description: Project values, code-responsibility expectations and contribution guidelines. Required reading before opening a PR or report.

involved.heading: Get involved
involved.intro: Three places to engage with the project:

cards.title: 🐛 Report a bug
cards.description: GitHub Issues — include logs and reproduction steps.

cards.title: 💡 Suggest a feature
cards.description: GitHub Discussions — share ideas before opening a PR.

cards.title: 🔀 Submit a PR
cards.description: GitHub Pull Requests — read the Code of Conduct first.

support.heading: Support the project
support.introRich: If ProxMenux saves you time, the easiest way to help others discover it is to <starlink>give the repo a ⭐ on GitHub</starlink>. If you want to go further:
support.kofiOutro: — buys the maintainer a coffee and keeps development going.
